Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer in the Buddy Clean application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the customer entity.

phoneNumber.description: Customer's phone number used for authentication and contact.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed by a customer for a cleaning service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the order entity.

serviceDate.description: The date the service is scheduled for.

totalAmount.description: The total amount for the order.

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., Razorpay, Cash on Delivery).

status.description: The status of the order (e.g., pending, confirmed, completed, cancelled).

variant.description: The variant selected for this service. (e.g. 1BHK, 5-seater)

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a cleaning service offered by Buddy Clean.

id.description: Unique identifier for the service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., Home Cleaning, Car Wash).

imageUrl.description: URL of the image representing the service.

basePrice.description: The base price of the service.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: Represents a contact message submitted through the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the contact message entity.

name.description: The name of the person submitting the contact message.

email.description: The email address of the person submitting the contact message.

message.description: The content of the contact message.

AdminRole.title: AdminRole
AdminRole.description: Represents an administrator role within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the admin role entity (Firebase Auth UID).

isAdmin.description: Indicates whether the user has administrator privileges.

definition.description: Stores customer profile information. The customerId must be the Firebase Auth UID, enforcing path-based ownership.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the customer.

definition.description: Stores orders for each customer, ensuring users can only access their own orders. Includes denormalized 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the customer.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Stores details for each cleaning service offered.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact message.

definition.description: Identifies administrators by their Firebase Auth UID. Existence of a document grants admin privileges.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure data security, scalability, and ease of management for the Buddy Clean application. It follows the principle of Authorization Independence by denormalizing necessary authorization data.  Specifically, the `orders` subcollection includes the `customerId`, thus enforcing ownership, making security rules simpler, and supporting atomic operations.

*   **Customers Collection (`/customers/{customerId}`):** Stores customer profiles. The `customerId` matches the Firebase Auth UID, ensuring that only the authenticated user can access their own profile data (Path-Based ownership). This structure naturally supports the QAP because listing is only allowed within the user's own path.
*   **Orders Subcollection (`/customers/{customerId}/orders/{orderId}`):** A subcollection under each customer document stores the customer's orders.  This enforces a strict parent-child relationship, ensuring that orders can only be created, read, updated, or deleted within the context of a specific customer.  This uses Path-Based ownership and provides Authorization Independence because the `customerId` is present in both the path and the document.
*   **Services Collection (`/services/{serviceId}`):** Stores the details of each cleaning service offered. This is a top-level collection where all service documents share the same security requirements, enabling secure `list` operations (QAP).  Since the services are managed via the admin panel, the rules can be configured to restrict write access to admin users only.
*   **Contacts Collection (`/contacts/{contactId}`):** Stores contact form submissions.  The documents in this collection share the same security posture, allowing for secure `list` operations if needed, ensuring QAPs are followed.
*   **Admin Roles Collection (`/roles_admin/{uid}`):** Uses the Existence over Content pattern for managing admin roles. The existence of a document with the user's UID in this collection grants admin privileges. This approach simplifies the rules by removing the need to inspect the document's content.

This design promotes Authorization Independence by avoiding the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which would introduce hierarchical authorization dependencies.  The use of path-based ownership (`/customers/{customerId}/orders/{orderId}`) and dedicated collections for different access requirements (`/services`, `/contacts`, `/roles_admin`) ensures that security rules are straightforward and easy to debug. The structure also supports QAPs by ensuring that collections with potentially sensitive data are secured appropriately, and that `list` operations can be performed safely without exposing unauthorized data.
